records.Notes: The characters “BCF”
Length of the “text” field, including the terminating NUL character
VCF format header text, NUL-terminated
Data length from CHROM to the end of INFO
Data length of FORMAT and individual genotype fields
Given as an offset into the mandatory contig dictionary
0-based leftmost coordinate

Length of the record as projected onto the reference sequence.
                        Must be the maximum of the length of the REF allele and the lengths
                        inferred from the SVLEN/LEN of any symbolic alleles
Variant quality; 0x7F800001 for a missing value
The number of INFO fields in this record
The number of REF+ALT alleles in this record

The number of samples in this record, stored as a three byte little-endian value.
                        Note that n_sample must be equal to the number of samples in the header
The number of FORMAT keys.  See <ref>
Variant identifier; 0x07 for a missing value

the first allele is REF (mandatory) followed by n_alleles - 1 ALT
                                             alleles, all encoded as typed strings

a vector of integer offsets into dictionary, one for each FILTER field value.
                                      “.” is encoded as MISSING

n_info pairs of typed vectors.
                                   The first value must be a typed atomic integer giving the offset of the INFO field
                                   key into the dictionary.
                                   The second value is a typed vector giving the value of the field
see below
Format key as an offset into the dictionary

Typing byte of each individual value, possibly followed by a typed int for the vector length.  
In effect this is the same as the typing value for a single vector, but for genotype values it appears only once before the array of genotype field values

The information of each individual is concatenated in the vector.  Every value is of the same fmt type.
Variable-length vectors are padded with END_OF_VECTOR values; a string is stored as a vector of char

records.Meaning:
The number of elements of the upcoming type. 
For atomic values, the size must be 1. 
If the size is set to 15, this indicates that the vector has 15 or more elements, and that the subsequent BCF2 byte stream contains a typed Integer indicating the true size of the vector. 
If the size is between 2–14, then this Integer is omitted from the stream and the upcoming stream begins immediately with the first value of the vector.
A size of 0 indicates that the value is MISSING.

records.Corresponding atomic type: A MISSING value without an explicit type provided

headers: String type with inline size of 4 followed by ACAC in ASCII

records.string with overflow size: overflow size encoded as INT8 with value 27

headers: 8 bit integer vector with overflow size

records.X dictionary offset: each value is a 2 element INT8 value
